front: I resumed my French lessons. (completed past action)
back: J'ai repris mes cours de français.
note: Passé composé = present of avoir/être + past participle, for completed past actions. Most verbs take avoir. reprendre is irregular (like prendre -> pris): past participle = repris. 'un cours' = a class/lesson.

source.should_be: J'ai repris mes cours de français.

front: It's going well. (how things are going)
note: Set phrase for 'it's going well', from se passer (to happen/go). Literally 'it happens itself well.' Handy for plans, work, the day: 'Comment ça se passe ?' = how's it going?
Possessive adjectives agree with the NOUN, not the owner: mon (m.sg), ma (f.sg), mes (plural, both genders). So 'my lesson' = mon cours, 'my lessons' = mes cours.
pour = for / in order to. pour + noun ('pour ça' = for that) OR pour + infinitive ('pour apprendre' = in order to learn) expresses purpose. utiliser = to use (regular -er): j'utilise.

source.you_wrote: [I am using Claude to do it.]

note: In French typography the marks ! ? : ; take a (non-breaking) space BEFORE them. Write 'Bonjour Rachael !', not 'Bonjour Rachael!'.
